quote: The heart was made to be broken.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: Each man kills the thing he loves.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: The heart will break, but broken live on.
author: Lord Byron

quote: After great pain, a formal feeling comes—
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: She had the perpetual sense, as she watched the taxi cabs, of being out, out, far out to sea and alone.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: All happy families resemble one another; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: He stepped down, trying not to look long at her, as if she were the sun, yet he saw her, like the sun, even without looking.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: I wasn't actually in love, but only a little curious.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quote: When you are sorrowful look again in your heart, and you shall see that in truth you are weeping for that which has been your delight.
author: Khalil Gibran

quote: The unhappy are egoistic, spiteful, unjust, cruel, and less capable of understanding each other than fools.
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: Where there is sorrow there is holy ground.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: Unable are the loved to die, for love is immortality.
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart and try to love the questions themselves.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

quote: Rummaging in our souls, we often dig up something that ought to have lain there unnoticed.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: It's a funny thing coming home. Nothing changes. Everything looks the same, feels the same, even smells the same. You realize what's changed is you.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quote: How much better is silence; the coffee cup, the table. How much less demanding.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: Your pain is the breaking of the shell that encloses your understanding.
author: Khalil Gibran

quote: Life does not cease to be funny when people die any more than it ceases to be serious when people laugh.
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: I measure every Grief I meet with narrow, probing Eyes—
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: Let everything happen to you: beauty and terror. Just keep going. No feeling is final.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

quote: That it will never come again is what makes life so sweet.
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: I am made and remade continually. Different people draw different words from me.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: If you look for perfection, you'll never be content.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: You talk when you cease to be at peace with your thoughts.
author: Khalil Gibran

quote: Man is what he believes.
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: I am not afraid of storms, for I am learning how to sail my ship.
author: Louisa May Alcott

quote: Forever is composed of nows.
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: One must be something in order to do something.
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: She felt… how life, from being made up of little separate incidents which one lived one by one, became curled and whole like a wave which thrusts one up one time and falls on the beach.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: No man is rich enough to buy back his past.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: Fare thee well! and if forever, still forever, fare thee well!
author: Lord Byron

quote: When you part from your friend, you grieve not; for that which you loved most in them is clearer in the absence.
author: Khalil Gibran

quote: So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quote: I thought how unpleasant it is to be locked out; and how worse it must be to be locked in.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: The Heart asks Pleasure—first— / And then—Excuse from Pain—
author: Emily Dickinson

quote: There is no greatness where there is not simplicity, goodness, and truth.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: I wish I had done everything on earth with you.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quote: In secret we met—In silence I grieve, That thy heart could forget, Thy spirit deceive.
author: Lord Byron

quote: Wait quietly for the next revelation.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

quote: The two most powerful warriors are patience and time.
author: Leo Tolstoy

quote: Every saint has a past, and every sinner has a future.
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: So we'll go no more a roving so late into the night.
author: Lord Byron

quote: You may forget but let me tell you this: someone in some future time will think of us.
author: Sappho

quote: I can only note that the past is beautiful because one never realizes an emotion at the time.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: Yet it is in our idleness, in our dreams, that the submerged truth sometimes comes to the top.
author: Virginia Woolf

quote: I realized that in love there was neither logic nor law.
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: You are the finest, loveliest, tenderest, and most beautiful person I have ever known—and even that is an understatement.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

quote: The deeper that sorrow carves into your being, the more joy you can contain.
author: Khalil Gibran

quote: It was not Death, for I stood up, And all the Dead, lie down—
author: Emily Dickinson
